title: mentha × rubra var. pratensis
intro: Mentha × rubra var. pratensis, also known as Red Mint or Wild Mint, is a perennial herb belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties, making it effective in treating digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Mentha × rubra var. pratensis include its ability to relieve nausea, improve digestion, and reduce stress, as well as its antimicrobial properties that can help combat infections. Herbal preparations of Red Mint, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
